github.name: Cognitive-Core-Skills
github.description: A universal, industry-neutral taxonomy of cognitive core skills (perception, memory, reasoning, planning, action, verification, learning, governance) for LLMs, SLMs, AI agents, and world models — with schemas, 159 skill cards, benchmarks, and CI.

github.name: Awesome-Scientific-LLM-Benchmarks
github.description: A curated, accuracy-first list of benchmarks for evaluating LLMs on scientific reasoning and discovery — math, physics, chemistry, materials, biology, and agentic science.

github.name: qwen-lora-finetune
github.description: Fine-tune Qwen2.5-Coder for code generation on your own dataset using QLoRA. Train on JSONL instruction-response pairs and produce a shareable ~16 MB LoRA adapter.

github.name: mpesa_safaricom-fraud_anomaly_detection
github.description: Real-time fraud detection engine for M-Pesa mobile money — Kafka/Flink streaming, ML-based risk scoring, and explainable multi-signal checks (velocity, SIM swap correlation, mule networks) feeding an interaction-aware decision engine, with a full production pipeline from ingestion through dbt analytics.

github.name: Machine-Learning
github.description: Collection of Machine Learning, Statistics, and Data Science notebooks covering EDA, hypothesis testing, regression, classification, clustering, ensemble learning, and association rule mining using Python.

github.name: fortress
github.description: Stealth Chromium engine that stops scrapers and browser agents from getting blocked, with one line of code change.

github.name: creatorhub
github.description: 多平台内容监控·采集·搬运 —— 纯 Python(FastAPI + Playwright),一个 Web 面板管起抖音 / 小红书 / 快手

github.name: 9router-v2
github.description: Self-hosted AI gateway — one endpoint, many providers, auto-fallback. OpenAI-compatible REST API with Cloudflare Workers AI automation.

github.name: Homekit
github.description: Homekit gives any AI agent direct, physical control over Apple Home. Flip lights. Lock doors. Read sensors. Through a single open interface.

github.name: OmniPost-AI
github.description: AI-powered Chrome extension for generating and publishing posts to Facebook, Threads, and X using ChatGPT or Gemini.

trends: The current tech landscape is heavily characterized by the rapid evolution and deployment of advanced AI, particularly Large Language Models (LLMs) and intelligent agents. There's a strong emphasis on evaluating and enhancing the 'cognitive core skills' of these AI systems, extending beyond basic text generation to complex reasoning, scientific discovery, and specialized tasks like code generation and 3D understanding. Agentic AI, capable of interacting with the physical world (smart homes) and digital environments (browser automation, content publishing), is gaining significant traction. This rise in agentic capabilities also brings critical security and privacy concerns, as evidenced by vulnerabilities leading to data leaks and broader discussions around digital surveillance. Alongside AI's proliferation, there's a strong parallel trend in developer productivity and automation, focusing on streamlining multi-platform content management, building flexible AI service routing (AI gateways), and personal effectiveness strategies like 'locking in' to combat 'time blindness'. Overall, the ecosystem is moving towards more intelligent, autonomous, and integrated systems, while simultaneously grappling with the ethical and security implications of such powerful technologies.

ideas.title: Cognitive Skills Playground for AI Agents
ideas.description: Develop a web-based platform where developers can upload or integrate their AI agent's API and evaluate its 'cognitive core skills' against a predefined taxonomy (perception, memory, reasoning, planning, action, verification, learning, governance). The platform would provide a suite of standardized tests and scenarios for each skill, giving granular feedback and a score. This helps developers understand their agent's strengths and weaknesses, allowing for targeted improvements. It could also include a leaderboard for different agent architectures or fine-tuned LLMs.
tech_stack: LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Qwen via custom interface)
PostgreSQL (for storing results and user data)
steps: Design and implement a database schema for skill taxonomies, test cases, and evaluation results.
Build a backend API (FastAPI) to manage user agents, run evaluations, and store outcomes.
Develop a module for each cognitive skill category, including specific test prompts and expected outputs/validation logic.
Integrate with popular LLM APIs (e.g., OpenAI, Qwen) and provide an interface for users to register their custom agent endpoints.
Create a frontend (React/Vue) for users to upload/configure agents, trigger evaluations, and view detailed reports/benchmarks.
Implement a scoring and reporting mechanism to visualize agent performance across different skills.

ideas.title: AI-Enhanced Multi-Platform Content Orchestrator with Stealth Browser Automation
ideas.description: Build a self-hosted or SaaS application that streamlines content creation, scheduling, and multi-platform publishing. Users can define content strategies, and the integrated AI generates text, image prompts, or even video script ideas. The system then uses 'stealth browser automation' (leveraging techniques from 'fortress') to publish this content across platforms like Facebook, X, Threads, TikTok, and other Chinese social media sites (like those mentioned in 'creatorhub'), bypassing common bot detection mechanisms. It could also monitor basic engagement metrics and feed them back to the AI for iterative content improvement.
tech_stack: OpenAI/Gemini APIs (or a custom AI gateway like '9router-v2' for flexibility)
OAuth/API keys for social media integrations
steps: Set up a Python backend with FastAPI for API endpoints and business logic.
Integrate with multiple social media platform APIs for publishing and basic analytics.
Develop a core AI generation module using OpenAI/Gemini, allowing users to define content types and tones.
Implement a robust 'stealth browser automation' layer using Playwright/Selenium, incorporating strategies to avoid bot detection, possibly drawing inspiration from 'fortress'.
Create a content scheduling and queueing system using Redis/Celery.
Design and build a user-friendly web interface for content management, scheduling, and performance monitoring.
Add a proxy or gateway layer similar to '9router-v2' to allow users to specify their preferred AI model provider or even local LLMs.

ideas.title: Privacy-First Local AI Home Assistant with Agent Action Governance
ideas.description: Develop a privacy-focused, self-hosted AI assistant that extends 'Homekit' capabilities. Instead of relying purely on cloud LLMs, it uses a local Small Language Model (SLM) for sensitive commands and offers a 'permission gateway' for AI actions. This gateway, inspired by security concerns like 'GitLost', would require explicit user confirmation for critical actions (e.g., 'unlock door', 'disable alarm') or provide clear logging and undo capabilities. It would integrate seamlessly with existing smart home ecosystems (like Apple Home) but place user control and data privacy at its core.
tech_stack: Homekit API / Homebridge (for smart home integration)
Local SLM/LLM (e.g., Llama.cpp for inference on Raspberry Pi/local server)
Custom API Gateway (similar to '9router-v2' concept, for routing requests to local/external LLMs)
Web interface (minimalist React/Vue or even a TUI) for configuration and action approval
SQLite (for local data storage, logs, and preferences)
steps: Set up a base environment on a local device (e.g., Raspberry Pi) capable of running a local SLM/LLM.
Integrate with the Homekit API or a Homebridge instance to control smart home devices.
Implement a local LLM inference server (e.g., using `llama.cpp` Python bindings) for processing commands.
Develop an 'Action Governance' module that intercepts AI-generated commands, categorizes their risk, and triggers user confirmation for high-risk actions.
Create a simple user interface (web-based or mobile app) for configuring the assistant, viewing action logs, and approving critical commands.
Build a custom 'AI Gateway' proxy that intelligently routes natural language queries to either the local SLM (for privacy/speed) or an external LLM (for complex queries).
Focus on robust error handling and clear logging for all AI interactions and device controls.

quick_actions: Experiment with fine-tuning a small, domain-specific LLM (e.g., Qwen2.5-Coder using QLoRA) on your own codebase or project documentation to generate contextual code snippets or explanations.
Allocate a dedicated 'lock-in' block of 90 minutes tomorrow, free from distractions and notifications, to tackle a complex coding problem or learn a new tech, and reflect on its productivity impact.

Research and set up a local AI gateway (like `9router-v2`) or a simple API proxy for your personal projects to manage multiple LLM providers, implement fallback mechanisms, or route requests to a local LLM.
Spend 30 minutes conceptualizing how you would implement a 'permission system' for an AI agent if it had access to your sensitive files or smart home devices, identifying potential safeguards.
